Commissioll 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 
(916) 322-5662 
William P. McNames 
Deputy City Attorney 
City of Garden Grove 
P.O. Box 3070 
Garden Grove, CA 
92642 
Dear Mr. McNames: 
Administratian •• 
executive/legal 
•• 
Enfarcement 
•• Statements of Economic Interest 
322.5660 
322·5901 
322~1 
322-6«4 
March 20, 1985 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-85-037 
Thank you for your letter requesting written advice on the 
requirements for reporting individual retirement accounts 
(IRAs). 
You specifically requested assistance in explaining to 
your councilmembers the rationale behind reporting lRAs. 
You indicated in your letter that you had received a copy 
of our January 1985 Bulletin which contained general reporting 
obligations on IRAs. 
In that bulletin, we explained that IRAs 
invested solely in mutual funds registered with the SEC or some 
other type of investment exempt from disclosure, e.g. treasury 
bills, savings accounts, would not be reportable. 
There are: however, certain types of IRAs known as 
"self-directed" IRAs. 
With those IRAs, the filer makes the 
investment decisions, and the interests held by the IRA are of 
the type that would require disclosure, e.g. common stock, real 
property. 
We require disclosure of these interests in a manner 
similar to that required of interests held through a business 
entity. 
A filer must disclose reportable investments or real 
property held by an IRA if the filer has a 10 percent or greater 
interest in the IRA. 
To provide a further rationale, you can remind your 
councilmembers that the definition of "investment" includes any 
financial interest held directly, indirectly or beneficially by 
the filer or his or her immediate family. 
(Government Code 
Section 82034.) 
Investments held by an IRA in which the filer 
has a 10 percent or greater interest are held indirectly. 


William P. McNames 
March 20, 1985 
Page 2 
I hope this provides you with the informa'tion you need to 
assist your councilmembers. 
If I can be of additional help, 
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
JET:plh 
Sincerely, 
, ;

Jeanette E. Turvill 
Legal Assistant 
Legal Division

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY 
(714) 638-6881 
February 13, 1985 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Legal Division 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
SUBJECT: 
Individual Retirement Account (IRA) Reporting Requirements 
on Economic Interest Report (Schedule A - Investments) 
Gentlemen: 
This letter is written pursuant to our conversation of January 24, 1985 
regarding the above referenced matter in which you confirmed our research, 
that the IRA reporting requirements are not set forth in statute, code or 
regulation. 
You also advised the reporting requirements would be the subject 
of a future regulation to be promulgated by the F.P.P.C. 
We presently have requests from two City Council members for guidance 
in their respective Economic Interest Statement Form 730 Schedule A 
disclosures. Reporting of IRA's may have an impact upon a number of other 
City personnel, and has and will raise a number of questions from designated 
employees as to the requirements for reporting IRA's. 
We, therefore, ask from 
you written advise on the reporting requirements for purposes of explanation 
to City Council members, members of various commissions and designated 
employees, the reasons and rationals underlying the determination as to what 
interests are reportable or unreportable. 
We are presently in receipt of your January 1985 Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division Bulletin and Form 730 Schedule A Instructions, attached. 
Respectfully, 
Attachments 
5124G

REPORTING INDIVIDUAL RETIREMENT ACCOUNTS (IRAS) 
If a filer has an investment interest in an Individual 
Retirement Account worth more than $1,000, he/she must report the 
IRA on Schedule A unless the IRA is totally invested in a mutual 
fund registered with the SEC or some other type of investment 
exempt from disclosure on Schedule A. 
In addition, he/she must 
report any investment interest worth more than $1,000 held 
through the IRA unless: 
. 
-
the file1;1 s funds are pooled with those of other 
investors, . and 
. 
the filer's interest in the IRA, or in any particular 
investment interest held by the IRA, is less than 10%. 
The filer. does not have to report investments held by an 
IRA if those investments are exempt from disclosure on Schedule A 
(e.g. insurance .policies). 
NEW REQUIREMENT: 
REPORTING PARTNERSHIPS 
If a filer has an investment of more than $1,000 in a 
limited or general partnership with 35 or fewer partners and 
there are only one or 
~wo general partners, the filer rr~y be 
required to list an ir.lestment in those general partne:s as well 
as listing his/her int.stment in the partnership. 
Foz~~pecific 
advice, you should corbet the FPPC.

sections.question: Your Request for Advice - Requirements for reporting individual retirement accounts (IRAs) on Economic Interest Statements, with specific focus on explaining the rationale behind reporting IRAs to councilmembers.
sections.conclusion: A filer must disclose reportable investments or real property held by an IRA if the filer has a 10 percent or greater interest in the IRA. IRAs invested solely in mutual funds registered with the SEC or other exempt investments (e.g. treasury bills, savings accounts) are not reportable. Self-directed IRAs where the filer makes investment decisions and holds reportable interests (e.g. common stock, real property) must be disclosed. Investments held by an IRA in which the filer has a 10 percent or greater interest are held indirectly and therefore fall within the definition of 'investment' under Government Code Section 82034.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the reporting requirements for Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) on Economic Interest Statements (Schedule A - Investments), and what is the rationale for these requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that filers must report investments held through self-directed IRAs if they have a 10% or greater interest in the IRA and the investments are of a type requiring disclosure (e.g., stocks, real property), but IRAs invested solely in SEC-registered mutual funds or exempt investments need not be reported. The rationale is that such interests are held indirectly by the filer and fall within the statutory definition of 'investment.'
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request (February 13, 1985 letter from City of Garden Grove) and FPPC response (March 20, 1985 advice letter). Extracted information from the FPPC response portion. Also contains bulletin text on IRA and partnership reporting requirements. Some OCR artifacts present in scanned document.

QUESTION: Your Request for Advice - Requirements for reporting individual retirement accounts (IRAs) on Economic Interest Statements, with specific focus on explaining the rationale behind reporting IRAs to councilmembers.

CONCLUSION: A filer must disclose reportable investments or real property held by an IRA if the filer has a 10 percent or greater interest in the IRA. IRAs invested solely in mutual funds registered with the SEC or other exempt investments (e.g. treasury bills, savings accounts) are not reportable. Self-directed IRAs where the filer makes investment decisions and holds reportable interests (e.g. common stock, real property) must be disclosed. Investments held by an IRA in which the filer has a 10 percent or greater interest are held indirectly and therefore fall within the definition of 'investment' under Government Code Section 82034.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the City of Garden Grove's request for written advice on IRA reporting requirements for Economic Interest Statements, clarifying that self-directed IRAs with reportable investments must be disclosed if the filer has a 10% or greater interest, while IRAs invested solely in exempt investments like SEC-registered mutual funds are not reportable.
